action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 card, 4 buttons, 1 soap

Your secret value function is:

card: 7, button: 0, soap: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I value the card the most (7 points), followed by the soap (3 points), and the buttons are worthless to me (0 points). Since I only have one opponent, I should aim to get the card for myself. To ensure we both get a positive score (which is better than zero if our proposals don't match), I should offer the opponent something they might value, or simply propose a split where I get the card and the opponent gets the soap and buttons. However, since I don't know the opponent's values, the safest bet to maximize my own score while ensuring a successful agreement is to propose getting the card and leaving the other items for them. If they value the soap or buttons, they will agree. If they value the card more, they might counter, but I can adjust.

Let's start by proposing that I take the card and the opponent takes the soap and buttons. This gives me the highest possible value item immediately.

Card: 7, Button: 0, Soap: 3

I will propose:
[Proposal: 1 card]

Wait, the rules say "If your proposals are complementary... Otherwise, both of you get zero points." This implies we need to reach an agreement where the items are fully divided. If I just say "1 card", and the opponent says "1 card", we have a conflict and both get 0. If I say "1 card" and they say "1 soap, 4 buttons", then we have a full division and we both get points.

So, my first move should be to claim the item I value most, while implicitly leaving the rest for the other player.

Message to opponent:
I want the card. Please take the soap and the 4 buttons.

Since I need to communicate clearly without using square brackets (which trigger the secret proposal format), I will send a direct message.

Message:
I want the card. Please take the soap and the 4 buttons.
action.label: response

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

Your secret value function is:

card: 4, button: 1, soap: 2

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
